title: Ransomware Attack
description: A ransomware attack was detected across the IROrg network after multiple endpoints executed suspicious binaries, initiated encoded PowerShell activity, and connected to malicious external infrastructure. Shortly afterward, the file server srv‑web01 began encrypting data using the .df_locked extension, accompanied by ransom notes in affected directories. Initial compromise originated from user workstations and progressed via credential misuse, leading to lateral movement and deployment of the ransomware payload. This case tracks the investigation, containment, and recovery actions following the incident.
tasks:
[{"createdBy": "responder1@irorg.com", "createdAt": "24/02/2026 15:19", "title": "Eradication Phase", "description": "Removed malicious binaries (update_213.exe, svchost32.exe, credscan.exe, df.exe) from affected hosts using endpoint protection remediation tools.
Conducted deep scanning using EDR and offline malware removal tools to verify no remaining persistence mechanisms (scheduled tasks, registry run keys, WMI event subscriptions, etc.).
Cleared malicious PowerShell scripts and disabled any unauthorised remote management channels established during compromise.
Ensured all compromised credentials (including admin_ir) were fully reset with MFA enforced.
Patched vulnerable systems and validated that no new suspicious outbound connections were being established to previously seen C2 infrastructure.", "owner": "responder1@irorg.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "24/02/2026 15:19", "endDate": "24/02/2026 15:19"}, {"createdBy": "responder1@irorg.com", "createdAt": "24/02/2026 14:43", "title": "Detection Phase", "description": "=== TASK ACTIVITY HISTORY ===

ID: ~45136
Author: responder1@irorg.com
Date: 24/02/2026 14:46
Type: case_task_log
Message: Summary of Actions Performed:

Reviewed SIEM alerts from multiple user workstations (ws-user03, ws-user04) indicating suspicious binary execution from %TEMP% directories, encoded PowerShell activity, and outbound connections to known malicious infrastructure.
Identified initial dropper execution (update_213.exe) and beaconing behavior from svchost32.exe correlating with C2 domains such as servicewikii.com and IP 88.119.174.129.
Confirmed abnormal administrative authentication from a user workstation to domain controller dc01.irorg.local, suggesting credential compromise.
Detected rapid file write activity and encryption events on file server srv-web01 involving the ransomware module df.exe and evidence of .df_locked encrypted files.
Verified employee reports that shared folders were inaccessible and ransom notes had appeared, confirming an active ransomware event.", "owner": "responder1@irorg.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "24/02/2026 14:43", "endDate": "24/02/2026 14:46"}, {"createdBy": "responder1@irorg.com", "createdAt": "24/02/2026 14:44", "title": "Analysis Phase", "description": "=== TASK ACTIVITY HISTORY ===

--- LOG ENTRY ---
ID: ~20624
Author: responder1@irorg.com
Date: 24/02/2026 14:47
Type: case_task_log
Message: Summary of Actions Performed:

Collected and analysed all provided IoCs, including malicious hashes, C2 domains, IP addresses, and encryption tool (df.exe).
Pivoted on file hashes using Cortex analysers (VirusTotal, HybridAnalysis, AbuseIPDB) to validate known malware families and identify external correlations.
Assessed lateral movement likelihood by reviewing credential misuse evidence (admin_ir login from compromised host).
Reviewed backup server logs from srv-backup01, identifying suspicious outbound traffic to 95.179.219.169, indicating potential data staging or exfiltration attempts.
Mapped activity to MITRE ATT&CK techniques (T1059 PowerShell, T1070 Log Clearing, T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, etc.) to profile the likely threat actor group.
Concluded that the attack pattern resembles behavior commonly associated with organised ransomware operations involving credential theft, internal reconnaissance, and controlled deployment of the encryption payload.", "owner": "responder1@irorg.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "24/02/2026 14:47", "endDate": "24/02/2026 15:09"}, {"createdBy": "responder2@irorg.com", "createdAt": "24/02/2026 14:46", "title": "Post Incident Activity", "description": "Post Incident Activity

ID: ~65616
Author: responder2@irorg.com
Date: 24/02/2026 15:23
Type: case_task_log
Message: Completed full incident documentation including timelines, IoCs, affected assets, and response actions in TheHive.
Shared all validated IoCs with the organisation’s MISP instance, assigning correct categories (Network Activity, Payload Delivery, Malware Hash, etc.).
Conducted a lessons-learned session to identify gaps in detection, credential hygiene, endpoint isolation, and backup resiliency.
Recommended implementation of network segmentation, improved backup validation procedures, and enhanced SIEM detection rules for early bin-execution anomalies.
Proposed strengthening MFA coverage and conducting user awareness training focused on malware execution vectors.
Formally closed the case after confirming no further malicious activity and that business services were fully operational.", "owner": "responder2@irorg.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "24/02/2026 15:23", "endDate": "24/02/2026 15:23"}, {"createdBy": "responder2@irorg.com", "createdAt": "24/02/2026 14:46", "title": "Recovery", "description": "Recovery phase.

ID: ~32912
Author: responder1@irorg.com
Date: 24/02/2026 15:18
Type: case_task_log
Message: Evidence Gathering

Captured volatile evidence (RAM, running processes, active network connections) from infected endpoints before network isolation.
Exported key Windows Event Logs (Security, System, PowerShell, Sysmon) from compromised systems to prevent log loss during containment activities.
Collected copies of malicious binaries (update_213.exe, svchost32.exe, df.exe) and suspicious scripts identified on affected hosts.
Recorded network connection metadata for outbound communication to known C2 infrastructure.
Ensured all captured evidence was hashed and securely stored for the full forensic analysis to be completed in the Analysis phase.


--- LOG ENTRY ---
ID: ~40996984
Author: responder1@irorg.com
Date: 24/02/2026 15:12
Type: case_task_log
Message: Summary of Actions Performed:

Isolated compromised endpoints (ws-user03, ws-user04) from the network via EDR containment controls to prevent further lateral movement.
Disabled affected AD accounts and initiated password resets for any users that show signs of credential abuse.
Blocked all identified malicious domains and IP addresses at the firewall and proxy layers (servicewikii.com, vc.myobtain.com, etc.).
Stopped active encryption process df.exe on srv-web01 and preserved volatile memory for forensic follow-up.
Prevented access to infected shared folders to limit propagation and preserve evidence.
Performed containment on the backup server to stop outbound traffic, pending forensic validation of backup integrity.", "owner": "responder1@irorg.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "24/02/2026 15:12", "endDate": "24/02/2026 15:22"}]
